title: Are We Entangled? | HG Amitasana Prabhu
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: When we are under the influence of Maya, we think that we are the doer of activities,
we think that we are active, we think that we are making the choices. But here Prabhupada
mentions that being under the grip of material nature or Maya, we are in one sense passive.
Although active, we are passive. That's why there is a verse in the Gita, one who sees
action in inaction and inaction in action as they are. Passive in the sense because
material nature is goading us, is forcing us to act in a particular way. We see plethora
of activities in this Gita.
In this world people are acting in different ways, some are very very short tempered, some
are good natured, some are kind, loving, wicked, cruel, all kinds of people. Actually many
times we decide I should not get angry, I should not behave like this, I should be more
gentle.
But our nature overpowers us. So that's why here Prabhupada mentions that under the influence
of modes of material nature which is forcing us to act at times in sinful ways, this is
the same question which Arjuna also asked. What is it that we end up acting in ways which
later on we regret, as if there is some kind of a force. So here he says, Prabhupada says,
his acts are influenced by the quality of nature. He says that the nature of nature
overpowers us. So that's why here Prabhupada mentions that under the influence of modes
of material nature, he says that us are already bound.
Penetrating a number of發
of material nature, proves that we must simply not get caught up in way of material nature,
the entire time these capacities of material nature and he becomes passive.
So in that sense, we all are bound. Although we may appear free, we may appear independent,
saying some affirmations how by imagining things but the laws of nature
are very very stringent and Krishna in the Gita gives us a clue in fact why clue
directly he says if we have to come out of the influence of material nature
maam eva ye prapadyante maya metam tarantite the reason why we have fallen
in the clutches of Maya is because of we've rebelled against the Lord just like
if someone violates the state laws he's punished in the recent we have been
hearing about the ball tampering scandal so there are some rules of fair play and
if you violate the rules you want to take undue advantage then you get
penalized so we see in this world how rules apply likewise the material nature
has stringent rules and knowingly unknowingly we violate those rules we
come out of the influence of the law and we are punished and there is no punishment
under the clutches of Maya.
So now in this verse we are getting a hint, Prabhupada is giving us, what is the solution?
Devahuti is asking, in fact the beauty of Bhagavatam is forget about finding a solution,
we don't even know the problem.
So here it is said, how can there be freedom for a soul as long as material nature acts
on him and binds him?
We don't even know that we are bound, forget about finding out what is the solution.
So Devahuti is asking this wonderful question, how can there be freedom for soul who as long
as the material nature acts on him, we know that material nature acts on us, all of us
are in the influence of Maya and binds him, what is the solution?
How can the soul extricate itself from this bondage?
This is a very nice verse which I came across, I thought it's relevant in this particular
context.
This verse says, patanga matanga kurunga brunga, meena hata pancha bhi reva pancha.
It's a very nice verse, we have heard this example earlier.
Patanga, patanga means moth.
Many times we have seen, during the rainy season if there is some bright light, the
sun is shining.
All the moths, they get helplessly drawn towards that fire or that bright light.
They cannot resist.
Morning time when we see these moths are all lying dead.
So do they not, every animal, every insect has a natural instinct not to die, to protect.
It's a natural instinct.
Prabhupada.
Prabhupada says four things are common between human beings and animals, eating, sleeping,
mating and defending, to protect.
You take an ant, ant is going, you just put a finger in front of it, it will turn.
It senses a danger and tries to go away.
The moth, I am sure they have that much intelligence to protect themselves, but when there is some
fire or there is some light, they are helplessly drawn.
And in fact the body starts getting heated.
They can feel the sense of burning.
But that fire is so intoxicating, the sight of that fire, the eyes, it cannot resist the
dancing flames of fire.
And then it hovers around it and drops dead.
